name: VIEMED HEALTHCARE, INC.
business: Viemed provides home medical equipment (HME) and post-acute healthcare services in the United States, focusing on respiratory, chronic care, and women's health products and services. The company's primary service offerings include home ventilation, sleep therapy, oxygen therapy, and healthcare staffing, targeting patients in all 50 states. As of December 31, 2025, it employed 1,382 permanent employees and served 12,259 active ventilator patients.

exposures.sentence: Viemed provides respiratory and other home medical equipment solutions (primarily through monthly rental arrangements), including home ventilation (invasive and non-invasive), BiPAP (bi-level positive airway pressure) and CPAP (continuous positive airway pressure) devices, percussion vests, oxygen concentrators, and other medical equipment.
Viemed provides healthcare staffing and recruitment services, offering tailored workforce solutions to external healthcare institutions and partners seeking qualified clinical professionals.
Viemed's care programs are designed specifically to treat patients in the home for less total cost and with a superior quality of care.

catalysts.event: CMS finalized a new National Coverage Determination (NCD) for noninvasive positive pressure ventilation (NIPPV) in the home for COPD
catalysts.sentence: On June 9, 2025, CMS finalized a new NCD establishing clear medical necessity criteria for NIPPV in the home for treatment of chronic respiratory failure related to COPD.

catalysts.event: One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) signed into law
catalysts.sentence: The federal budget reconciliation legislation, known as the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA), signed into law on July 4, 2025, introduces a broad set of statutory and policy changes that may affect the healthcare industry and our operations.
the Lehan acquisition completed on July 1, 2025
On June 6, 2025, the Company's Board of Directors authorized and approved a share repurchase program.

catalysts.event: 2025 share repurchase program completed and terminated
catalysts.sentence: Under the terms of the 2025 Share Repurchase Program, the Company repurchased 1,976,441 of its common shares and the program was completed and terminated during the three months ended September 30, 2025.
On March 4, 2026, the Company's Board of Directors authorized and approved a share repurchase program.

catalysts.event: Third Amendment to Senior Credit Facilities extended delayed draw term loan commitment expiration
catalysts.sentence: On November 7, 2025, the Company entered into a Third Amendment to the 2022 Senior Credit Facilities that, among other things, further extended the delayed draw term loan commitment expiration date from November 29, 2025 to November 29, 2026

catalysts.event: CMS announced 6-month nationwide temporary moratorium on Medicare enrollment of certain DMEPOS supplier types
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, CMS announced the imposition of a 6-month nationwide temporary moratorium on the Medicare enrollment of certain DMEPOS medical supply company supplier types, with the stated objective of combating fraud, waste, and abuse.
We last renewed our accreditation with Medicare in August 2024, meaning our next renewal is scheduled for August 2027.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. home healthcare spending projected to reach $250 billion by 2031 with CAGR of approximately 7%
tamClaims.sentence: Projections from industry analysts indicate that U.S. home healthcare spending will increase, reaching $250 billion by 2031, with a CAGR of approximately 7%.

tamClaims.claim: Respiratory devices market projected CAGR of approximately 6%
tamClaims.sentence: Analysts in the industry anticipate a consistent and robust growth trajectory, projecting Compound Annual Growth Rates ("CAGR") of approximately 6% for respiratory devices and 8% for OSA devices.

tamClaims.claim: OSA devices market projected CAGR of approximately 8%
tamClaims.sentence: Analysts in the industry anticipate a consistent and robust growth trajectory, projecting Compound Annual Growth Rates ("CAGR") of approximately 6% for respiratory devices and 8% for OSA devices.
